fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 67. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 64. FLAG (2026-07-24 editorial wave 7): no confirmed edition since 2019 (postponed 2020-21 for COVID, Crossroads Tremblant festival cancelled 2022 with no evidence of a promised 2023+ return) — every dimension score here (organization=4, prestige=2, field_depth=3, etc.) was set against a currently non-bookable event. Recommend a human decide whether this profile should carry an active-style score at all, matching the birkebeinerrittet-road precedent for lapsed events. Numbers left untouched per no-silent-reshuffle rule.

course_description.character: IMPORTANT — no confirmed edition has run since 2019. When it operated, this was a paved-road cyclosportive through the Laurentians based at Tremblant Resort, part of the wider Crossroads Tremblant cycling festival alongside separate MTB and Gravel Fondo events. Four distances were offered in 2019: Super Fondo (160 km), Gran Fondo (125 km), Medio Fondo (80 km) and Molo Fondo (45 km). The Nordet portion of the route was singled out for scenery. The format leaned toward personal-challenge riding with ride leaders managing pace groups, rather than a single-field competitive race, though selected KOM segments provided a timed/competitive element.
course_description.signature_challenge: Historically, the Nordet section of the Laurentians route — the scenic core of the ride, no longer verifiable as current given the event's lapsed status.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Numerous hotels and resorts with participant discounts via Tourisme Mont-Tremblant historically offered discounts during the event; not verified as currently applicable given no confirmed edition since 2019.

history.origin_story: First edition staged September 7, 2013, per the City of Mont-Tremblant's technical-support agreement for that year. Later folded into the broader Crossroads Tremblant festival (Gran Fondo, Gravel Fondo, and MTB events run together under one umbrella) organized with Cycling Canada / FQSC support.
notable_moments: 2019: Most recent confirmed edition, May 26 — four distances (160/125/80/45 km)
2022: Crossroads Tremblant festival (including Gran Fondo) formally cancelled; organizer stated an intention to return in 2023, but no evidence of a 2023-2026 edition was found

biased_opinion.summary: There is no evidence Gran Fondo Mont-Tremblant has run since May 26, 2019. It was a real, well-regarded Laurentians cyclosportive — police-escorted group pace riding, four distances from 45 to 160 km, roughly 1,500-2,000 riders at its peak, folded into the Crossroads Tremblant festival alongside MTB and Gravel Fondo events. COVID postponed 2020 and 2021, the entire 2022 Crossroads festival was then cancelled outright, and the organizer's stated intention to return in 2023 has no supporting evidence of ever happening. Mont-Tremblant's current event calendar lists Salsa Tremblant on the exact August weekend this database's prior file claimed for Gran Fondo Mont-Tremblant — that date appears to be contamination from an unrelated destination event, not evidence of a revival.
strengths: Historically strong Laurentians scenery and a well-regarded pace-group format that welcomed first-timers alongside more competitive KOM-chasers
Four distances (45/80/125/160 km) gave a genuine range of entry points
Police-supported closed-enough roads and an established resort-town base at Tremblant
weaknesses: No confirmed edition since 2019 — this is not a currently bookable event
The broader Crossroads Tremblant festival it belonged to was cancelled in 2022 with no verified return
A rival Mont-Tremblant event (Salsa Tremblant) now occupies the August date this profile previously claimed, suggesting date contamination rather than a live listing
Never a fully competitive, ranked gran fondo — it was closer to a supported personal-challenge ride with optional timed segments
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Treat this as dormant, not bookable. If you want the Laurentians cycling experience Gran Fondo Mont-Tremblant once offered, look for a currently confirmed Quebec sportive rather than this one.

final_verdict.one_liner: Laurentians classic on ice since 2019 — no confirmed return after the 2022 Crossroads Tremblant cancellation.
final_verdict.should_you_race: No — there is no current edition to register for. If a 2027+ edition is ever announced, revisit; until then, choose an active Quebec or Ontario sportive instead.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a currently active Quebec road sportive, check the FQSC (Fédération québécoise des sports cyclistes) event calendar; for a similarly resort-based mountain ride, consider an active Whistler or Vermont-region gran fondo.

videos.title: Discover cycling in the Mont-Tremblant region
videos.description:
The Gran Fondo Mont-Tremlant brought us to the region 130 km northwest of Montreal. We went back, not only to participate in the big event, but to explore the great roads and to dine at some of the great restaurants in the Mont-Tremblant area.

CYCLING NEWS/FEATURES/VIDEOS: http://cyclingmagazine.ca/

FOLLOW US!
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/canadiancycling/
Twitter: https://twitter.com/CDNCyclingMag
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/cyclingmag?fref=ts

quotes.text: You feel like you're in a mini Tour de France here with the police in front there's that experience that prestige and my reward is when cyclists cross the finish line and are happy about their experience and the way their pack behaved safe fluid and at the right speed.

terrain_notes.text: Beautiful roads with beautiful scenery throughout the Mont-Tremblant region.

race_day_tips.text: Good chance to challenge yourself in September after riding all summer, with police security allowing you to ride through red lights safely.
Safe environment for first-timers to learn about riding in groups without worrying about traffic.

additional_quotes.text: My reward is when cyclists cross the finish line and are happy about their experience and the way their pack behaved safe fluid and at the right speed and this is how we cater to close to 2000 cyclist

rider_intel.search_text: Gran Fondo Mont-Tremblant is a mass cycling event in the beautiful Mont-Tremblant region of Quebec that caters to close to 2000 cyclists. The event offers two distances: 80k for beginners and those new to the sport, and 125k for more challenging riding. Riders describe beautiful roads with stunning scenery throughout the region. The event provides police security that allows cyclists to ride safely through red lights without worrying about traffic. Feed stations provide drinks and food along the route. The atmosphere is described as having Tour de France prestige with police escorts, creating a safe and fun environment for both experienced riders and first-timers to learn group riding skills. Local communities are welcoming, with station workers expressing amazement at seeing so many cyclists together. The event concludes with food, friends, drinks and a big party celebration, following the traditional Italian gran fondo format of bringing the cycling community together.

Last confirmed edition May 26, 2019; 2020-21 postponed for COVID; entire 2022 Crossroads Tremblant festival cancelled with no evidence of a 2023-2026 return. The file's prior August 21-22, 2026 date belongs to the unrelated Salsa Tremblant event, not Gran Fondo Mont-Tremblant.
